name: textual-serve

metadata.name: textual-serve
metadata.summary: Turn your Textual TUIs in to web applications

documentation:
# textual-serve Every [Textual](https://github.com/textualize/textual) application is now a web application. With 3 lines of code, any Textual app can run in the browser.

This is Posting running in the terminal.

This is Posting running in the browser.
--- ## Getting Started First, [install (or upgrade) Textual](https://textual.textualize.io/getting_started/#installation). Then install `textual-serve` from PyPI: ``` pip install textual-serve ``` ## Creating a server First import the Server class: ```python from textual_serve.server import Server ``` Then create a `Server` instance and pass the command that launches your Textual app: ```python server = Server("python -m textual") ``` The command can be anything you would enter in the shell, as long as it results in a Textual app running. Finally, call the `serve` method: ```python server.serve() ``` You will now be able to click on the link in the terminal to run your app in a browser. ### Summary Run this code, visit http://localhost:8000 ```python from textual_serve.server import Server server = Server("python -m textual") server.serve() ``` ## Configuration The `Server` class has the following parameters: | parameter | description | | -------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | command | A shell command to launch a Textual app. | | host | The host of the web application (defaults to "localhost"). | | port | The port for the web application (defaults to 8000). | | title | The title show in the web app on load, leave as `None` to use the command. | | public_url | The public URL, if the server is behind a proxy. `None` for the local URL. | | statics_path | Path to statics folder, relative to server.py. Default uses directory in module. | | templates_path | Path to templates folder, relative to server.py. Default uses directory in module. | The `Server.serve` method accepts a `debug` parameter. When set to `True`, this will enable [textual devtools](https://textual.textualize.io/guide/devtools/). ## How does it work? When you visit the app URL, the server launches an instance of your app in a subprocess, and communicates with it via a websocket. This means that you can serve multiple Textual apps across all the CPUs on your system. Note that Textual-serve uses a custom protocol to communicate with Textual apps. It *does not* simply expose a shell in your browser. There is no way for a malicious user to do anything the app-author didn't intend. ## See also See also [textual-web](https://github.com/Textualize/textual-web) which serves Textual apps on a public URL. You can consider this project to essentially be a self-hosted equivalent of Textual-web.

functions.name: textual_serve.server.to_int
documentation:
Convert to an integer, or return a default if that's not possible.

Args:
    number: A string possibly containing a decimal.
    default: Default value if value can't be decoded.

Returns:
    Integer.

classes.name: textual_serve.server.Server

methods.name: textual_serve.server.Server.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    app_factory: A callable that returns a new App instance.
    host: Host of web application.
    port: Port for server.
    statics_path: Path to statics folder. May be absolute or relative to server.py.
    templates_path" Path to templates folder. May be absolute or relative to server.py.

methods.name: textual_serve.server.Server.initialize_logging
documentation:
Initialize logging.

May be overridden in a subclass.

methods.name: textual_serve.server.Server.request_exit
documentation: Gracefully exit the app.

methods.name: textual_serve.server.Server._make_app
documentation:
Make the aiohttp web.Application.

Returns:
    New aiohttp web application.

methods.name: textual_serve.server.Server.handle_download
documentation: Handle a download request.

methods.name: textual_serve.server.Server.on_shutdown
documentation:
Called on shutdown.

Args:
    app: App instance.

methods.name: textual_serve.server.Server.on_startup
documentation:
Called on startup.

Args:
    app: App instance.

methods.name: textual_serve.server.Server.serve
documentation:
Serve the Textual application.

This will run a local webserver until it is closed with Ctrl+C

methods.name: textual_serve.server.Server.handle_index
documentation:
Serves the HTML for an app.

Args:
    request: Request object.

Returns:
    Template data.

methods.name: textual_serve.server.Server._process_messages
documentation:
Process messages from the client browser websocket.

Args:
    websocket: Websocket instance.
    app_service: App service.

methods.name: textual_serve.server.Server.handle_websocket
documentation:
Handle the websocket that drives the remote process.

This is called when the browser connects to the websocket.

Args:
    request: Request object.

Returns:
    Websocket response.
Serve a Textual app.

modules.name: textual_serve._binary_encode
documentation:
An encoding / decoding format suitable for serializing data structures to binary.

This is based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bencode with some extensions.

The following data types may be encoded:

- None
- int
- bool
- bytes
- str
- list
- tuple
- dict

functions.name: textual_serve._binary_encode.dump
documentation:
Encodes a data structure in to bytes.

Args:
    data: Data structure

Returns:
    A byte string encoding the data.

functions.name: textual_serve._binary_encode.load
documentation:
Load an encoded data structure from bytes.

Args:
    encoded: Encoded data in bytes.

Raises:
    DecodeError: If an error was encountered decoding the string.

Returns:
    Decoded data.

classes.name: textual_serve._binary_encode.DecodeError
documentation: A problem decoding data.

variables.name: textual_serve.download_manager.DOWNLOAD_CHUNK_SIZE
documentation: 64 KB

class_variables.name: textual_serve.download_manager.Download.app_service
documentation: The app service that the download is associated with.

class_variables.name: textual_serve.download_manager.Download.delivery_key
documentation: Key which identifies the download.

class_variables.name: textual_serve.download_manager.Download.file_name
documentation:
The name of the file to download. This will be used to set
the Content-Disposition filename.

class_variables.name: textual_serve.download_manager.Download.open_method
documentation: The method to open the file with. "browser" or "download".

class_variables.name: textual_serve.download_manager.Download.mime_type
documentation: The mime type of the content.

class_variables.name: textual_serve.download_manager.Download.encoding
documentation:
The encoding of the content.
Will be None if the content is binary.

class_variables.name: textual_serve.download_manager.Download.name
documentation: Optional name set bt the client.

class_variables.name: textual_serve.download_manager.Download.incoming_chunks
documentation:
A queue of incoming chunks for the download.
Chunks are sent from the app service to the download handler
via this queue.

classes.name: textual_serve.download_manager.DownloadManager

methods.name: textual_serve.download_manager.DownloadManager.create_download
documentation:
Prepare for a new download.

Args:
    app_service: The app service to start the download for.
    delivery_key: The delivery key to start the download for.
    file_name: The name of the file to download.
    open_method: The method to open the file with.
    mime_type: The mime type of the content.
    encoding: The encoding of the content or None if the content is binary.

methods.name: textual_serve.download_manager.DownloadManager.download
documentation:
Download a file from the given app service.

Args:
    delivery_key: The delivery key to download.

methods.name: textual_serve.download_manager.DownloadManager.chunk_received
documentation:
Handle a chunk received from the app service for a download.

Args:
    delivery_key: The delivery key that the chunk was received for.
    chunk: The chunk that was received.

methods.name: textual_serve.download_manager.DownloadManager._get_app_service
documentation:
Get the app service that the given delivery key is linked to.

Args:
    delivery_key: The delivery key to get the app service for.

methods.name: textual_serve.download_manager.DownloadManager.get_download_metadata
documentation:
Get the metadata for a download.

Args:
    delivery_key: The delivery key to get the metadata for.

methods.name: textual_serve.download_manager.DownloadManager.cancel_app_downloads
documentation:
Cancel all downloads for the given app service.

Args:
    app_service_id: The app service ID to cancel downloads for.

Class which manages downloads for the server.

Serves as the link between the web server and app processes during downloads.

A single server has a single download manager, which manages all downloads for all
running app processes.

classes.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService.stdin
documentation: The processes standard input stream.

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService._build_environment
documentation:
Build an environment dict for the App subprocess.

Args:
    width: Initial width.
    height: Initial height.

Returns:
    A environment dict.

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService._open_app_process
documentation:
Open a process to run the app.

Args:
    width: Width of the terminal.
    height: height of the terminal.

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService.encode_packet
documentation:
Encode a packet.

Args:
    packet_type: The packet type (b"D" for data or b"M" for meta)
    payload: The payload.

Returns:
    Data as bytes.

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService.send_bytes
documentation:
Send bytes to process.

Args:
    data: Data to send.

Returns:
    True if the data was sent, otherwise False.

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService.send_meta
documentation:
Send meta information to process.

Args:
    data: Meta dict to send.

Returns:
    True if the data was sent, otherwise False.

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService.set_terminal_size
documentation:
Tell the process about the new terminal size.

Args:
    width: Width of terminal in cells.
    height: Height of terminal in cells.

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService.blur
documentation: Send an (app) blur to the process.

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService.focus
documentation: Send an (app) focus to the process.

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService.stop
documentation: Stop the process and wait for it to complete.

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService.run
documentation:
Run the Textual app process.

!!! note

    Do not call this manually, use `start`.

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService.on_data
documentation:
Called when there is data.

Args:
    payload: Data received from process.

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService.on_meta
documentation:
Called when there is a meta packet sent from the running app process.

Args:
    data: Encoded meta data.

methods.name: textual_serve.app_service.AppService.on_packed
documentation:
Called when there is a packed packet sent from the running app process.

Args:
    payload: Encoded packed data.

Creates and manages a single Textual app subprocess.

When a user connects to the websocket in their browser, a new AppService
instance is created to manage the corresponding Textual app process.
